pi_items.source: Computed from real food.gdt data using Python statsmodels OLS regression for auxiliary regression: food_exp ~ const + income + yhat_log
Computed from real food.gdt data using Python statsmodels OLS regression for auxiliary regression: food_exp ~ const + income + yhat_log
Computed from real food.gdt data using Python statsmodels OLS regression for auxiliary regression: food_exp ~ const + l_income + yhat_linear
Computed from real food.gdt data using Python statsmodels OLS regression for auxiliary regression: food_exp ~ const + l_income + yhat_linear
Downloaded from POE5 (Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition) data repository at principlesofeconometrics.com. File contains 40 observations with variables food_exp and income.
Matches path specification in task description and setup script - cannot verify without running task execution

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is real and sourced from the POE5 (Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition) repository, containing 40 household observations with weekly food expenditure (food_exp) and weekly income (income) variables. Analysis of the Davidson-MacKinnon J-test using this real data reveals significantly different coefficients and t-statistics than claimed in metadata: Auxiliary Linear regression coefficient is 0.1060 (not 0.30) with t-stat 0.1490 (not 1.5); Auxiliary Log regression coefficient is 0.9028 (not 0.72) with t-stat 1.3181 (not 3.6). All four numerical predictions in metadata are contradicted by the actual data.
